Content: Schafer: Oh, here's a copy of my notebook for Grim Fandango.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，這是我做《Grim Fandango》時的筆記本。

Content: And then here's me trying to figure out the name of the game.
Content_zh: 然後這是我在想遊戲名稱。

Content: Dead Leads. Deadly Deeds.
Content_zh: Dead Leads。Deadly Deeds。

Content: Deeds of Death. Death's Deeds.
Content_zh: Deeds of Death。Death’s Deeds。

Content: You know, I read my old names to you because Grim Fandango was such a great name that it was funny to see all the bad names,
Content_zh: 我把以前想過的名字唸給你們聽，是因為《Grim Fandango》這名字太棒了，所以回頭看那些爛名字很好笑，

Content: but right now I don't have a name for this game, so all these names, it's just sad.
Content_zh: 可是我現在還沒替這款遊戲取名字，所以看到這些名字只覺得難過。

Content: I did make a nice chart, though.
Content_zh: 不過我倒是做了一張不錯的表。

Content: Puzzles on it.
Content_zh: 上面有謎題。

Content: Don't show that.
Content_zh: 別拍那個。

Content: This is a horrible process, by the way.
Content_zh: 順帶一提，這個過程真的很糟。

Content: It's just not going to have, we're going to end up with some terrible name like Stacking.
Content_zh: 最後大概會取到什麼超爛的名字，像《Stacking》那樣。

Content: Just kidding, Lee.
Content_zh: 開玩笑的，Lee。

Content: [Music] Schafer: OK. So we'll start the email thread about this.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：好，那我們就開一串 email 討論這件事。

Content: Thanks for everyone hustling to get the build running.
Content_zh: 謝謝大家趕工把版本跑起來。

Content: It worked great.
Content_zh: 效果很好。

Content: I was very proud of it.
Content_zh: 我真的很以它為傲。

Content: It was awesome.
Content_zh: 超棒的。

Content: I hope no one's feelings got hurt. OK.
Content_zh: 希望沒有人受傷。好。

Content: Awesome. Thank you.
Content_zh: 太棒了，謝謝。

Content: Kipnis: I liked the little animation polish that Chris did on that.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我喜歡 Chris 在那裡做的那段小動畫潤飾。

Content: Choy: Do we just send our notes out? Is that what we do?
Content_zh: Choy：我們就把意見寄出去嗎？是這樣嗎？

Content: Schafer: Yeah Schafer: I like your cascading Post-its.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。Schafer：我喜歡你那串層層疊疊的便利貼。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Ron Gilbert.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：Ron Gilbert。

Content: Ron Gilbert, I've been pestering him to play the game and give us his feedback.
Content_zh: Ron Gilbert，我一直纏著他要他玩遊戲、給我們回饋。

Content: And he's been really busy but now The Cave is done and now he's going to play the game.
Content_zh: 他最近真的很忙，不過現在《The Cave》做完了，他要來玩這款遊戲了。

Content: He's going to play it.
Content_zh: 他要玩了。

Content: All the Cloud Colony stuff that we have today and then we're going to talk at 4:00.
Content_zh: 今天我們有的 Cloud Colony 內容他都會玩，然後四點我們再聊。

Content: He's going to tell me what he thought.
Content_zh: 他會告訴我他的想法。

Content: Offscreen: Why didn't he play the game during the HOF, because I saw him in the meeting.
Content_zh: 畫外音：他為什麼沒有在 HOF 時玩？因為我有看到他在會議裡。

Content: Schafer: Oh, he said he was going to play the game during the HOF, but when I said, I kind of got the impression he has not played it yet.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，他說他會在 HOF 時玩，但我後來有點覺得他其實還沒玩。

Content: I think he had a lot of email to read that day.
Content_zh: 我想他那天有很多 email 要看。

Content: He's been busy.
Content_zh: 他很忙

Content: He's not busy anymore.
Content_zh: 他不再忙了

Content: You know, he's made some of these games before.
Content_zh: 你知道，他以前做過一些這類遊戲。

Content: So he's got very particular things that he would do if it was his game that might drive him crazy if we didn't do them the exact same way.
Content_zh: 所以如果這是他的遊戲，他會有一些非常特定的做法；如果我們沒有照那樣做，可能會讓他抓狂。

Content: Schafer: So we'll find out.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們等著看。

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: What he'll think.
Content_zh: 他會怎麼想。

Content: He'll probably say, eh, it's fine. Whatever. Who cares.
Content_zh: 他大概會說：「嗯，還行啦。隨便。誰在乎。」

Content: All right.
Content_zh: 好吧

Content: Schafer: Thank you for playing.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝你玩。

Content: Do you have any general impressions of things you'd like to share?
Content_zh: 你有什麼整體感想想分享嗎？

Content: Gilbert: Yeah, I think my general impression was that, I mean, it's very interesting playing point-and-click games.
Content_zh: Gilbert：有，我覺得整體來說，玩點擊式冒險遊戲是很有趣的。

Content: There's a certain, there's a certain serenity to them, which is nice.
Content_zh: 它們有某種、有某種寧靜感，這點很好。

Content: You know, you just, I kind of felt like I could just lean back in my chair and I could just think.
Content_zh: 你知道，我就覺得自己可以往椅背一靠，慢慢思考。

Content: And there wasn't any pressure going on, which I kind of liked.
Content_zh: 而且沒有什麼壓力，這點我滿喜歡的。

Content: So I thought it was very, you know, enjoyable experience and then it kind of brought back, you know, some of those, you know,
Content_zh: 所以我覺得那是很愉快的體驗，也讓我想起那些，嗯，

Content: really old, you know, point-and-click feelings.
Content_zh: 很老派的點擊式冒險感覺。

Content: Schafer: And these are my notes from Ron's play-through.
Content_zh: Schafer：這些是我在 Ron 完整遊玩時做的筆記。

Content: Tram puzzle, he liked the tram puzzle.
Content_zh: 電車謎題，他喜歡電車謎題。

Content: He had four things that he liked and he had three pages other stuff.
Content_zh: 他有四件喜歡的事，另外有三頁其他東西。

Content: Gilbert: Do you want me to keep going?
Content_zh: Gilbert：你要我繼續嗎？

Content: 'Cause I'm onto the do-not-like's now.
Content_zh: 因為我現在要講不喜歡的了。

Content: Schafer: Oh no, you don't do any of those.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔不，那些就不用講了。

Content: Gilbert: OK, so I'm done.
Content_zh: Gilbert：好，那我講完了。

Content: Schafer: Wait. You've got four likes and then three pages of you-don't-likes.
Content_zh: Schafer：等等。你有四個喜歡，然後三頁不喜歡。

Content: Gilbert: And then three pages, well, you know...
Content_zh: Gilbert：然後三頁，你知道……

Content: Schafer: OK, let's go. Let's do it.
Content_zh: Schafer：好了 走吧 走吧

Content: Gilbert: I'm not happy gamer, I'm grumpy gamer. OK.
Content_zh: Gilbert：我不是快樂玩家，我是暴躁玩家。好。

Content: Things that I did not like.
Content_zh: 我不喜歡的地方。

Content: The dialogue trees didn't seem very focused to me.
Content_zh: 對話樹在我看來不夠集中。

Content: Falling through the clouds was super annoying.
Content_zh: 一直從雲上掉下去超煩。

Content: I thought the characters walked too slow.
Content_zh: 我覺得角色走路太慢了。

Content: Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：好

Content: Schafer: The exit arrows.
Content_zh: Schafer：出口箭頭

Content: He wanted exit arrows when you're just panning across the room, which I don't know if I...
Content_zh: 他希望在只是橫向移動畫面時也有出口箭頭，這點我不確定……

Content: I don't know if I agree with that one, but it's worth thinking about.
Content_zh: 我不知道我是否同意 但值得考慮

Content: And then I just had some ideas for dialogue while he was talking.
Content_zh: 然後他講話時，我也想到一些對話點子。

Content: Was that decisive looking?
Content_zh: 那是有決心的表情嗎？

Content: Schafer: Starting with some sad news.
Content_zh: Schafer：先從一個難過的消息開始。

Content: I think some of you saw the email went out that one of our co-workers is leaving
Content_zh: 我想你們有些人看到 email 了，我們有一位同事要離開。

Content: My old friend Ron Gilbert is going to be leaving Double Fine and pursuing his, it's a big round of boo's for Ron Gilbert.
Content_zh: 我的老朋友 Ron Gilbert 要離開 Double Fine，去追求他的……大家一起給 Ron Gilbert 噓聲。

Content: Boo.
Content_zh: 噓。

Content: [Laughter] Boo-urns.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Boo-urns。

Content: We don't handle good-byes well, Ron, here.
Content_zh: Ron，我們這裡不太會好好道別。

Content: To pursue his lifelong dream of not working at Double Fine.
Content_zh: 追求他一生的夢想 不去 Double Fine 工作

Content: [Laughter] Ron will be leaving pretty soon, like today.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Ron 很快就要離開了，大概今天。

Content: He doesn't like long drawn-out emotional good-byes, so he wanted to just leave and not just linger on.
Content_zh: 他不喜歡拖很久、情緒很重的告別，所以他想直接走，不想一直耗著。

Content: But yeah, do you have a long speech that you'd like to make at this point?
Content_zh: 不過，你現在有什麼長篇演說想發表嗎？

Content: Gilbert: I don't.
Content_zh: Gilbert：沒有。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: And with those words, [Applause]
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：說完這幾個字之後，[掌聲]

Content: Schafer: He left.
Content_zh: Schafer：他走了

Content: Offscreen Voice: You knew he was leaving, you didn't tell anybody.
Content_zh: 畫外音：你早就知道他要走，卻沒告訴任何人。

Content: Schafer: Just a little while.
Content_zh: Schafer：也才一小段時間。

Content: I mean, we'd talked about it like, pretty honest about, you know, him coming to the company, doing a game.
Content_zh: 我是說，我們談得滿坦白的，像是他來公司、做一款遊戲。

Content: What are you going to do after?
Content_zh: 你之後要做什麼？

Content: He didn't know.
Content_zh: 他不知道

Content: He, Ron's pretty independent and has always, you know, more naturally is kind of a loner, rebel.
Content_zh: Ron 很獨立，他一直都比較像天生的獨行俠、反叛者。

Content: Dangerous.
Content_zh: 危險

Content: Drifter.
Content_zh: 漂泊者。

Content: And you can't keep him in a cage, man.
Content_zh: 你不能把他關在籠子裏

Content: Can't just contain Ron Gilbert.
Content_zh: 不能就這樣把 Ron Gilbert 關住。

Content: But we're sad to see him go.
Content_zh: 但看到他走我們很傷心

Content: I liked, I liked having Ron around.
Content_zh: 我喜歡，我喜歡有 Ron 在身邊。

Content: He's like my older brother in the games industry, kind of.
Content_zh: 他就像我在遊戲界的哥哥

Content: Yeah, he said, he said he would still, every time I sent him a build, he would play through it and give us some us some feedback
Content_zh: 對，他說之後每次我寄版本給他，他還是會完整玩過，給我們一些回饋，

Content: so hopefully we'll have him come back in or Skype with us from wherever he is.
Content_zh: 所以希望我們能請他回來，或是不管他在哪裡，都用 Skype 跟我們聊。

Content: Schafer: That's what he was going to do originally, was just play the game, give us feedback, help us out to give us some, some, his opinion about stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：這本來就是他一開始要做的事，就是玩遊戲、給我們回饋，幫忙提供一些他對各種東西的看法。

Content: And he said he'd still do that, so now we just don't have to pay him so it's kind of win/win.
Content_zh: 而他說他還是會做，所以現在我們不用付錢給他，算是雙贏。

Content: Gilbert: ok Schafer: Any last words?
Content_zh: Gilbert：好。Schafer：有什麼遺言嗎？

Content: Say, say good luck.
Content_zh: 祝你好運

Content: It's going to be great.
Content_zh: 會很棒的

Content: Say good luck, it's going to be great.
Content_zh: 說祝好運，會很棒的。

Content: Gilbert: Good luck, it's going to be great.
Content_zh: Gilbert：祝你好運，會很棒的

Content: Schafer: Well, thanks, Ron.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，謝啦，Ron。

Content: Gilbert: I really look forward to playing it again, Tim.
Content_zh: Gilbert：Tim，我真的很期待再玩一次。

Content: Schafer: Awesome, thanks, great. OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：太好了，謝謝，太好了。好

Content: [Music] Schafer: You're all over the new episode, man, let me tell you that.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：老兄，我跟你說，新的那集到處都是你。

Content: Schafer: Hello, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家好

Content: Hey, nice to see you all. Hello.
Content_zh: 很高興見到你們 你好

Content: Thank you for coming.
Content_zh: 謝謝你能來

Content: Rice: For Red's, we got, Game Informer is going to be doing a big spread on the game
Content_zh: Rice：Red’s 這邊，Game Informer 會替這款遊戲做一篇大篇幅報導，

Content: and they're coming by in a week and a half to talk with Tim and play the game and everything.
Content_zh: 他們一個半星期後會過來，跟 Tim 聊、玩遊戲等等。

Content: So it should be like a six-page spread coming out in April on that game, which is right after our trailer.
Content_zh: 所以四月應該會有一篇六頁的專題，剛好在我們的預告片之後。

Content: So we're starting on that now, too.
Content_zh: 所以我們現在也開始了

Content: But it's going to be busy PR times very soon.
Content_zh: 但很快就要忙公關了

Content: During GDC we're doing a Brutal book launch party, so that should be good.
Content_zh: GDC 期間我們會辦一場《Brütal Legend》書籍發表派對，應該會不錯。

Content: Everybody's invited to that.
Content_zh: 大家都被邀請去

Content: And we'll get you details on that, too.
Content_zh: 我們也會告訴你這些細節的

Content: Lots of things.
Content_zh: 很多事

Content: Rice: So that kind of stuff has been happening now.
Content_zh: Rice：這種事已經發生了

Content: We have this, this kind of schedule for our first little chunk of P R and I'm sure there'll be more down the line as the game
Content_zh: 我們現在有第一小段 PR 行程，我相信隨著遊戲越來越接近推出，後面還會有更多。

Content: actually gets closer to coming out.
Content_zh: 越來越接近出來

Content: But this is more just a, for people who had never heard about Kickstarter or not anything about the game,
Content_zh: 但這比較像是讓那些從沒聽過 Kickstarter，或不太知道這款遊戲的人，

Content: this'll be kind of the first time they hear about the game as a game instead of the game as a product of the Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 第一次把它當成一款遊戲來認識，而不是 Kickstarter 的產物。

Content: Rice: How is everybody feeling about showing the game in a week and a half and doing a trailer in three weeks?
Content_zh: Rice：一個半星期後就要展示遊戲，三週後要做預告片，大家感覺怎麼樣？

Content: It's crazy.
Content_zh: 太瘋狂了

Content: People are going to be talking about the game.
Content_zh: 大家會開始談這款遊戲了。

Content: Schafer: It's easy, right?
Content_zh: Schafer：很簡單吧？

Content: Petty: I think it all hinges on how awesome the name is.
Content_zh: Petty：我想這都要看名字有多棒

Content: Petty: It's a small little tidbit.
Content_zh: Petty：這只是個小小的切入點。

Content: It's your window into the game.
Content_zh: 是大家看進遊戲的一扇窗。

Content: Rice: So it'll be actually, you know, announcing the name of the game.
Content_zh: Rice：所以實際上會宣布遊戲名稱。

Content: Doing a teaser trailer and then also doing some interviews with press and talking more in detail about what the game's actually about.
Content_zh: 做一支前導預告，然後也接受一些媒體訪問，更詳細聊遊戲到底是什麼。

Content: Petty: Ah, yeah, so.
Content_zh: Petty：啊，是的，所以。

Content: Well, there's a lot of tasks related to all that and I'd say it's probably going to take up two-thirds,
Content_zh: 嗯，相關工作很多，我會說這大概會吃掉美術團隊大多數人三分之二，

Content: easily two-thirds of the sprint for the majority of the art team.
Content_zh: 很輕鬆就會吃掉這個 sprint 的三分之二。

Content: Rice: No, I mean, it's a hard thing because no matter what, showing the game publicly is going to eat into the schedule somehow.
Content_zh: Rice：不是，我是說這很難，因為不管怎樣，公開展示遊戲一定會吃到排程。

Content: Whether it's a trailer or a demo.
Content_zh: 不管是預告片還是 demo

Content: Like there's always going to be work that's done specifically for that, that wouldn't have been done otherwise.
Content_zh: 總會有一些專門為了這件事而做的工作，不然原本是不會做的。

Content: But I think it's just a matter of you got to talk about your game and you have to like get out there.
Content_zh: 但我覺得重點就是你得談你的遊戲，得讓它出去見人。

Content: Otherwise you might make a great game, but nobody knows about it.
Content_zh: 不然你可能做出一款很棒的遊戲，卻沒有人知道。

Content: So it's just kind of give or take of let's figure out the points in the schedule where we're going to be able to insert these things with the least amount of pain,
Content_zh: 所以就是要取捨，找出排程中能用最少痛苦塞進這些事的時間點，

Content: but also to get like the biggest positive affect for the game.
Content_zh: 同時也替遊戲帶來最大的正面效果。

Content: And I think that just happened to be right now because this is when PAX is and GDC and all the big conventions.
Content_zh: 我覺得剛好就是現在，因為 PAX、GDC 和各大展會都在這段時間。

Content: And so we want to take advantage of those things falling in line with our dev cycle
Content_zh: 所以我們想利用它們剛好配合我們開發週期的時機，

Content: and try to figure out ways to have something at those events that people will be excited about while still minimizing the impact on production of the game.
Content_zh: 想辦法在那些活動上拿出能讓大家興奮的東西，同時盡量降低對遊戲製作的影響。

Content: Rice: So Game Informer stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：所以 Game Informer 的事。

Content: And they're obviously flying someone out here.
Content_zh: 他們顯然會派人飛過來。

Content: So we have to try to figure out a part of the game that they could play that would like hold up all right.
Content_zh: 所以我們得找出遊戲裡一段他們可以玩、而且狀態撐得住的內容。

Content: Schafer: I mean, Cloud Colony's was the thing we HOF'ed, so it's playable.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，Cloud Colony 是我們 HOF 過的內容，所以可以玩。

Content: Petty: So I think the thing we're needing on that for you and Tim or Tim and you to play through the Cloud Colony
Content_zh: Petty：所以我覺得我們需要你和 Tim，或 Tim 和你，把 Cloud Colony 玩一遍，

Content: and identify kind of what your demo loop is and see if there's any additional polish you want us to do.
Content_zh: 找出你的 demo 循環大概是什麼，看看還有沒有什麼額外潤飾要我們做。

Content: Rice: Yeah, well, so he's coming Wednesday, next Wednesday.
Content_zh: Rice：他下週三要來

Content: So I would imagine we want a good, at least half day of hammering from tests, just to make sure everything is... Kipnis: At least.
Content_zh: 所以我想至少要有半天讓測試狠狠跑一輪，確定一切都……Kipnis：至少。

Content: Rice: what we think it is.
Content_zh: Rice：我們想的是什麼

Content: Yeah, so I don't know.
Content_zh: 是啊，所以我不知道。

Content: Do you want to try to lock it down Monday night or do you want to lock it down to Tuesday afternoon?
Content_zh: 你想週一晚上鎖版，還是鎖到週二下午？

Content: I mean, he's not coming 'til afternoon Wednesday.
Content_zh: 他週三下午才來

Content: So we have Wednesday morning also.
Content_zh: 所以我們還有星期三早上

Content: Kipnis: Yeah, let's not, let's not, let's not take it to the wire like that.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，別，別，別把時間壓到那麼最後一刻。

Content: Let's just aim for Monday end of day, which may be like before Tuesday 10:00 a.m. or something.
Content_zh: 就以週一下班前為目標吧，可能也就是週二早上十點前之類的。

Content: Rice: I'll write down "end of day Monday".
Content_zh: Rice：我會寫下「週一下班前」。

Content: Kipnis: It's kind of what I'm working on right now, is making her movements be at a good speed and also, look right with animation.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我現在做的差不多就是讓她的移動速度剛好，動畫看起來也對。

Content: So this is her like running.
Content_zh: 這就是她像跑步一樣

Content: So like, you know, clicking kind of close by, she'll do a walk, but clicking sort of far away, she'll run over.
Content_zh: 像是點得比較近，她會用走的；點得比較遠，她就會跑過去。

Content: But the fact that she switches to and from, faster, travel method is good ultimately.
Content_zh: 但她會在比較快的移動方式之間切換，整體來說是好事。

Content: We just need it to look good.
Content_zh: 只是我們需要它看起來好看。

Content: It'll take a little bit of animation for that.
Content_zh: 這需要一點動畫

Content: Rice: Cool. All right.
Content_zh: Rice：酷 好吧

Content: I'm going to jump on that call, but this is good.
Content_zh: 我要接電話，但這很好

Content: It feels like it's not far off at all.
Content_zh: 感覺已經差不多了。

Content: Kipnis: No, no, it's not so bad.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，沒那麼糟。

Content: Rice: All right. Thanks, guys.
Content_zh: Rice：好吧 謝了

Content: OK, cool.
Content_zh: 好吧，酷。

Content: Rice: Water? Soda?
Content_zh: Rice：水？汽水？

Content: Turi: I'll grab a water.
Content_zh: Turi：我去拿水

Content: Rice: All right. Turi: Thanks.
Content_zh: Rice：好吧 謝謝你

Content: Rice: Have you been here before?
Content_zh: Rice：你來過這裡嗎？

Content: Rice: The nature of being in a magazine business is that they have a lot of readers, so they get a lot of big exclusives because they have such a, a wide reach
Content_zh: Rice：做雜誌的特性就是他們有很多讀者，所以他們很常拿到大型獨家，因為觸及很廣。

Content: and it's a place that people go often to announce their games or show really early stuff.
Content_zh: 人們也常去那裡宣布遊戲，或展示非常早期的內容。

Content: And so they're used to seeing busted builds and bugs and whatever.
Content_zh: 所以他們習慣看到壞掉的版本、bug 之類的東西。

Content: And...
Content_zh: 而且……

Content: Why is this ringing?
Content_zh: 為什麼會響？

Content: Double Fine.
Content_zh: Double Fine。

Content: Voice on Phone: Hi. I'm looking for Tim Schafer.
Content_zh: 電話聲音：嗨，我找 Tim Schafer。

Content: Schafer: That's our mobile kind of guy over there.
Content_zh: Schafer：那是我們的行動平台負責人

Content: This is our R&D group doing secret stuff.
Content_zh: 我們的研發團體在做祕密的事

Content: That's Oliver. He's our lead programmer.
Content_zh: 那是 Oliver 他是我們的主程式設計師

Content: Franzke: Nice to meet you. Turi: Nice to meet you, Tim.
Content_zh: Franzke：幸會 很高興見到你 Tim

Content: Kipnis: Hi, how's it going?
Content_zh: Kipnis：嗨，你好嗎？

Content: Turi: Good. Nice to meet you. I'm Tim from Game Informer.
Content_zh: Turi：很好 幸會 我是 Game Informer 的 Tim

Content: I just watched your side quest thing so, I know way more about you than you do me, but that's all right.
Content_zh: 我剛看了你的支線任務那集，所以我對你的了解比你對我的了解多很多，不過沒關係。

Content: Turi: You guys are talking, did you say that you're thinking, what is the release date, like the projected release date or window for...
Content_zh: Turi：你們剛剛在聊，你是說你們在想，發行日是什麼？預估發行日期或時程大概是……

Content: Schafer: Who's saying... Rice: We're still working on that.
Content_zh: Schafer：誰說的……Rice：我們還在規劃。

Content: Schafer: Later. Turi: Yeah, OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：以後 好的

Content: Schafer: Later this year.
Content_zh: Schafer：今年晚些時候

Content: Rice: Yeah, I mean it's hard 'cause it's still being designed and you know, still working on it.
Content_zh: Rice：是啊，我是說這很困難 因為它仍然在設計中，你知道的，還在努力

Content: Turi: They said, they said just holiday.
Content_zh: Turi：他們說，只說年底假期。

Content: But is it, is there any chance for that at all?
Content_zh: 但有機會嗎？

Content: Like with the...
Content_zh: 就像……

Content: Schafer: Getting this game done by the holiday?
Content_zh: Schafer：在年底假期前完成這遊戲？

Content: Can we get this game done by the holidays?
Content_zh: 我們能在年底假期前完成這個遊戲嗎？

Content: Rice: Up to you, man.
Content_zh: Rice：由你決定

Content: Schafer: So i'll click right here first, just to set things up.
Content_zh: Schafer：我先點這裡，把東西準備好。

Content: It's a point-and-click graphic adventure, Turi: Oh, you guys are excited about that
Content_zh: 這是一款點擊式圖像冒險遊戲，Turi：喔，你們對這個很興奮耶。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, remember that thing from the pitch video?
Content_zh: Schafer：對，記得那段影片嗎？

Content: So you walk around.
Content_zh: 所以你可以四處走動。

Content: You have an inventory down here to bring up.
Content_zh: 下面有物品欄可以叫出來。

Content: Right now, Turi: It's cool.
Content_zh: 現在，Turi：這很酷。

Content: It's cool as it always is, you know.
Content_zh: 一直都很酷

Content: I'm in a, I'm in a position of like responsibility when it comes to this.
Content_zh: 我在這件事上算是站在一個有責任的位置。

Content: And like there's a big responsibility that comes with checking the game out for the first time
Content_zh: 第一次看這款遊戲，背後有很大的責任。

Content: and I'm really excited to see what people have to say about this, because this is sort of a game out of time.
Content_zh: 我真的很期待看到大家會怎麼談它，因為這有點像是不合時代的遊戲。

Content: You know, it's something that's a relic, but it doesn't look like one.
Content_zh: 你知道，它像是某種遺物，但看起來又不像。

Content: But you know, the genre is something that hasn't been really around much outside of like The Cave and them some like indie games.
Content_zh: 可是這個類型，除了《The Cave》和一些獨立遊戲之外，其實已經不太常見了。

Content: I love having the responsibility of, you know, taking this experience with like Tim and Greg,
Content_zh: 我很喜歡能負起這個責任，和 Tim、Greg 一起體驗這些，

Content: checking out the game and presenting it to our readership, you know.
Content_zh: 看看遊戲，然後把它介紹給我們的讀者。

Content: So they can keep it on the radar or not.
Content_zh: 讓他們可以把這款遊戲放在雷達上，或不要。

Content: Schafer: I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道。

Content: I don't like showing the game ever before it's done.
Content_zh: 我從來不喜歡在遊戲完成前展示它。

Content: There's a, it's like people have to force me to show it.
Content_zh: 好像有人逼我展示

Content: Like I always joked that I would never show the game at all, except for to my family and that's only when it's done.
Content_zh: 我一直開玩笑說，除了給我的家人看，我根本不會展示遊戲，而且也只會在完成後才給他們看。

Content: But I wouldn't let them take the shrink wrap off it, ideally.
Content_zh: 但理想上，我連封膜都不會讓他們拆。

Content: Because it's never quite done.
Content_zh: 因為它永遠都還差一點。

Content: Schafer: And this is a preview; that's one thing.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是預覽，這是一件事。

Content: Previews are pretty usually pretty gentle on you.
Content_zh: 預覽通常對你還滿溫柔的。

Content: I mean, they normally don't be like oh, man, I just saw a sneak peek.
Content_zh: 我是說，他們通常不會說：「喔天啊，我剛看了一段搶先畫面。」

Content: This game's the worst I've ever seen.
Content_zh: 這遊戲是我見過最糟糕的

Content: They're usually like, yeah, they're working on it.
Content_zh: 他們通常都在努力

Content: Here's what they're working on and we hope it turns out cool, 'cause they'll give you the benefit of the doubt.
Content_zh: 這是他們正在做的東西，我們希望成果會很酷，因為他們會先給你一點信任。

Content: Then they send a different person in to review it, who's like, just a total dick.
Content_zh: 然後他們會派另一個人來評測，那個人就是個徹頭徹尾的混蛋。

Content: Especially at Game Informer.
Content_zh: 尤其是 Game Informer

Content: Schafer: So there you have it.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以就是這樣。

Content: Those are our kids.
Content_zh: 那些是我們的孩子。

Content: So we now, what do you think the best title is for this story?
Content_zh: 那你覺得這個故事最好的標題是什麼？

Content: Turi: Yeah, this is a difficult thing, probably to name, I assume.
Content_zh: Turi：對，這可能很難說出來

Content: Rice: We've got ideas.
Content_zh: Rice：我們有主意了

Content: Schafer: We ain't got nothing.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們什麼都沒有

Content: There was Young Monsters.
Content_zh: 有一個是 Young Monsters。

Content: Turi: OK. Schafer: Small Offerings.
Content_zh: Turi：好。Schafer：Small Offerings。

Content: Turi: Hey, I like that.
Content_zh: Turi：我喜歡

Content: Schafer: Broken Age.
Content_zh: Schafer：《Broken Age》

Content: Schafer: But yeah, so we're going to keep coming up with some ideas and we'll definitely have it to you by Wednesday. Turi: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：不過對，我們會繼續想點子，週三前一定會給你。Turi：好。

Content: Rice: Tuesday. Turi: Tuesday.
Content_zh: Rice：星期二 星期二。

Content: Always trying to push it.
Content_zh: 總是想再往後推。

Content: Always get one more day.
Content_zh: 總是想多爭取一天。

Content: Rice: I don't think you can stop the printing press.
Content_zh: Rice：我想你擋不住印刷機。

Content: Schafer: Usually I have the game named and the characters named before we talk to people.
Content_zh: Schafer：通常我和別人說話前 就有遊戲的名字和角色的名字

Content: So I don't know if the press is early or the game is late.
Content_zh: 我不知道媒體是早還是晚

Content: Probably the game is late.
Content_zh: 大概是遊戲晚了。

Content: But it's not that late.
Content_zh: 但還不算太晚

Content: It's great. Game is doing great.
Content_zh: 很好 遊戲做得很好

Content: Backers. Don't worry about it.
Content_zh: 贊助者們，別擔心。

Content: It's doing fine.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: Schafer: Here's another fun trick to do.
Content_zh: Schafer：再來一個有趣的把戲

Content: Young Monsters.
Content_zh: Young Monsters

Content: That font is terrible.
Content_zh: 那字型太可怕了

Content: This is the laziest box art ever.
Content_zh: 這是史上最懶的盒裝封面。

Content: Ah, that's hyphenated.
Content_zh: 啊，那是連字。

Content: Ah, fuck it.
Content_zh: 啊，算了。

Content: Schafer: I thought I'd come up with a great title this weekend, but I chose not to.
Content_zh: Schafer：我本來以為這週末會想出很棒的標題，但我選擇沒有。

Content: Crook: Did you come up with any bad ones?
Content_zh: Crook：你有想出什麼爛名字嗎？

Content: Schafer: And I could just name the damn kids, I could just call it the game whatever their names are.
Content_zh: Schafer：而且我乾脆替那兩個該死的小孩取名字，遊戲就用他們的名字好了。

Content: Like Banjo Kazooey.
Content_zh: 像《Banjo-Kazooie》。

Content: Ryken: I think you just need to spend a day dressed up halfway as a girl and halfway as a boy.
Content_zh: Ryken：我覺得你只需要花一天 打扮成半個女孩 半個男孩

Content: Schafer: Dream on.
Content_zh: Schafer：做夢吧。

Content: Ryken: It'll come to you.
Content_zh: Ryken：靈感就會來了。

Content: Schafer: I know what you're after.
Content_zh: Schafer：我知道你在找什麼

Content: Schafer: So the process was kind of grueling, 'cause the game's already so established,
Content_zh: Schafer：這個過程有點折磨，因為遊戲已經很成形了。

Content: it's always hard to name a game when it's already established.
Content_zh: 遊戲已經成形後，要取名字總是很難。

Content: It's a lot easier to do it before you've, before you've done anything, 'cause then the design kind of, you feel like you're just building on that name
Content_zh: 在什麼都還沒做之前取會簡單很多，因為那樣設計就像是建立在那個名字上，

Content: as opposed to attaching a name later.
Content_zh: 而不是後來才把名字貼上去。

Content: Petty: The reason why I'm really enjoying Tim's struggling with names is because I got a year of shit from Tim about the name Stacking.
Content_zh: Petty：我真的很享受 Tim 為名字苦惱，因為《Stacking》這個名字我被 Tim 嘲了一整年。

Content: And I never came up with anything better.
Content_zh: 我從沒想出過更好的辦法

Content: Schafer: I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道。

Content: This is the team's suggestions.
Content_zh: 這是團隊的建議

Content: The Distant Neighbor. A Girl and Boy.
Content_zh: The Distant Neighbor。A Girl and Boy。

Content: The Encounter. A Tale of Two.
Content_zh: The Encounter。A Tale of Two。

Content: Kindred Odyssey. The Unfinished Bridge.
Content_zh: Kindred Odyssey。The Unfinished Bridge。

Content: The Strange Offering. The Kind Double Creatures.
Content_zh: The Strange Offering。The Kind Double Creatures。

Content: The Kind Endangered Creatures. Mom Can Be Annoying.
Content_zh: The Kind Endangered Creatures。Mom Can Be Annoying。

Content: Get Off My Back. Divided Interest.
Content_zh: Get Off My Back。Divided Interest。

Content: Dude, Where's My Ship? Collision Course.
Content_zh: Dude, Where’s My Ship? Collision Course。

Content: Collision Curse. Similar Lines.
Content_zh: Collision Curse。Similar Lines。

Content: Deep Lines. Split Decision.
Content_zh: Deep Lines。Split Decision。

Content: True Lies. Collateral Damage. Predator.
Content_zh: True Lies。Collateral Damage。Predator。

Content: Schafer: It's hard to come up with space names, ask George.
Content_zh: Schafer：太空名字很難想，問 George 就知道。

Content: Greedo? Come on.
Content_zh: Greedo？拜託。

Content: Crook: Prune Face?
Content_zh: Crook：Prune Face？

Content: Franzke: Can I also ask, so the trailer, what is the actual, what's, what's up with that?
Content_zh: Franzke：我能問一下嗎 預告片 到底是怎麼回事

Content: Are we, are we planning on rendering that in game, what is the scope?
Content_zh: 我們是打算用遊戲內渲染嗎？範圍是什麼？

Content: Rice: So we, I talked with Lee and Tim about like a script for it and Majus is doing an animatic for that right now,
Content_zh: Rice：我有跟 Lee 和 Tim 聊過腳本，Majus 現在正在做動態腳本，

Content: so hopefully within the next few days we'll start seeing what that looks like.
Content_zh: 希望我們能在接下來的幾天內 開始看到它是什麼樣子

Content: Just so you guys can see what scenes and everything will be in it.
Content_zh: 只是讓你們看看裡面會有哪些場景之類的。

Content: Rice: Yeah, but it's just a matter of what we can pull off, right?
Content_zh: Rice：是的，但這只是我們能做的，對吧？

Content: And I think that for now, just talking about the trailer is probably the most time sensitive thing.
Content_zh: 我覺得現在談預告片大概是最有時間壓力的事。

Content: But I guess the idea is to try to get something that would be, you know, exciting enough for people to see onstage at GDC awards
Content_zh: 但我想這點子是要試著得到一些 令人興奮的東西 讓人們在 GDC 頒獎典禮上看到

Content: or just kind of a large event.
Content_zh: 或只是大事件

Content: Rice: The next big thing is the GDC thing.
Content_zh: Rice：下一件大事是 GDC 的事

Content: So Tim's hosting the award show and we've been working on a teaser trailer, like a short little 45 second thing
Content_zh: 所以 Tim 要主持頒獎典禮，我們一直在做一支前導預告，短短 45 秒左右，

Content: showing the actual game in motion for the first time.
Content_zh: 第一次展示實際遊戲動起來的樣子。

Content: Schafer: I was imaging we would take the approach of looking at all of our cut scenes
Content_zh: Schafer：我原本想像的是，我們可以看看所有過場動畫，

Content: and look at the ones that are farthest along and seeing if there's like three that can be polished or chunks of them.
Content_zh: 挑進度最前面的，看有沒有三段，或其中幾個片段可以打磨。

Content: Like things that are dramatic, like the girl falling through the sky or you know, things that we already have done somewhat.
Content_zh: 像是比較戲劇性的東西，例如女孩從天空掉下來，或是我們已經做了一部分的內容。

Content: I mean, I'm not against having shots where we show environments.
Content_zh: 我是說，我不反對放一些展示環境的鏡頭。

Content: Like pan of our environments.
Content_zh: 像是環境的平移鏡頭。

Content: Just if it's only that, that's an obvious like, maybe just 'cause I'm in the games industry, you know what that trailer is.
Content_zh: 只是如果只有那樣，就很明顯，可能只是因為我在遊戲業，你會知道那種預告片代表什麼。

Content: It's like we don't got nothing.
Content_zh: 就是「我們什麼都沒有」。

Content: Schafer: It's not yet a solved problem.
Content_zh: Schafer：還沒解決呢

Content: Like how to make people excited about adventure games.
Content_zh: 比如如何讓人們對冒險遊戲感到興奮

Content: So I can't tell you right now.
Content_zh: 所以我現在不能告訴你

Content: Rice: I think we should have Majus animatic it up, too, just to see how long, Schafer: Animatic it up?
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得也該讓 Majus 做成動態腳本看看，看看已經多長。Schafer：做成動態腳本？

Content: Rice: how long we're looking already.
Content_zh: Rice：看看我們目前大概多長。

Content: Schafer: That's the first time I've heard that used as verb.
Content_zh: Schafer：我第一次聽到這個詞被當動詞用。

Content: Rice: Animatic it up? Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：做成動態腳本？Schafer：對。

Content: Petty: Here, I'll put some notes together and we can send it to Majus.
Content_zh: Petty：我會整理一些筆記，然後我們可以寄給 Majus。

Content: [Skype rings] Fietzek: Hello. Offscreen Voice: Hi, Majus.
Content_zh: [Skype 鈴聲] Fietzek：哈囉。畫外音：嗨，Majus。

Content: Fietzek: So I really wanted to continue working in the Reds family.
Content_zh: Fietzek：我真的很想繼續在 Reds 這個系列裡工作。

Content: and so I talked with Greg and talked to Tim and it worked out, which is cool.
Content_zh: 於是我和 Greg 談過 和 Tim 談過了 這很不錯

Content: But it's boring, of course.
Content_zh: 但當然很無聊

Content: It's not as exciting as an office, of course.
Content_zh: 當然沒有辦公室那麼刺激

Content: Because in the office, it's so much excitement, pitching something to Tim.
Content_zh: 因為在辦公室裡，把東西提給 Tim 看會很刺激。

Content: And here it's, you know, like I'm sending it out and then I have to wait.
Content_zh: 這就像我要發出去 然後我必須等

Content: And then there's an email and emails are great and lovely and all but I really miss, of course, the office and all.
Content_zh: 然後會有 email，email 很棒、很可愛，但我當然真的很想念辦公室那些。

Content: Now I started working on the actual teaser trailer.
Content_zh: 現在我開始做真正的前導預告。

Content: But I just started.
Content_zh: 但我才剛開始

Content: And I sent it to Tim and I wonder what he thinks.
Content_zh: 我把它寄給了 Tim 我想知道他怎麼想的

Content: Schafer: This is Majus' animatic.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是 Majus 的動態腳本。

Content: [Music] Offscreen Voice: How do you like the trailer?
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：你覺得預告片怎麼樣？

Content: Schafer: I think it's going to be great.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得會很棒的

Content: It's, I mean, it's short but it's a teaser.
Content_zh: 它，我是說，它很短，但它是前導預告。

Content: Rice: So, it's looking rad.
Content_zh: Rice：所以，看起來超帥。

Content: I think it's almost exactly what we're hoping for, so that's rad.
Content_zh: 我覺得幾乎正是我們想要的樣子，所以很帥。

Content: Oh my goodness.
Content_zh: 天啊

Content: This is the worst.
Content_zh: 這是最糟糕的。

Content: He had to go back to Germany.
Content_zh: 他不得不回德國

Content: Gardner: Basically the first thing that I'm doing when I do the layout for one of our, for any sequence, is I'll play it.
Content_zh: Gardner：基本上我在替任何一段做 layout 時，第一件事就是播放它。

Content: And I'll be like, OK, so that, I'm going to use that background for the Cloud Colony, that animation.
Content_zh: 然後我會想，好，Cloud Colony 那段我要用那個背景、那個動畫。

Content: I'm going to use that background from the hole sequence, I'm going to use a floating animation.
Content_zh: 洞穴段落我要用那個背景，還要用一段漂浮動畫。

Content: That, I don't know how we're going to do but I'm going to figure that out later.
Content_zh: 我不知道我們該怎麼做 但我會想出辦法的

Content: This one, I know Ray is animating so I'll just, as soon as he's done, I'll just throw in his animation.
Content_zh: 這段我知道 Ray 在做動畫，所以等他做好，我就把他的動畫丟進去。

Content: Crook: So that's one of the shots that's in the, it's going to be in the trailer.
Content_zh: Crook：所以那是預告片裡會出現的其中一個鏡頭。

Content: So we'll be able to take the work that I've been doing, which is all game play stuff.
Content_zh: 這樣我們就能沿用我一直在做的內容，也就是那些 gameplay 的東西。

Content: And we'll just do a slight custom version of it to match up with the animatic.
Content_zh: 然後稍微做一個客製版本，讓它配合動態腳本。

Content: 'Cause sometimes, you know, Majus will do the animatic and it just isn't the way that it plays out exactly in the game.
Content_zh: 因為有時候 Majus 做的動態腳本，不會和遊戲裡實際演出的方式完全一樣。

Content: And so we'll just customize it a little bit.
Content_zh: 所以我們就把它客製一下

Content: So some of it might get thrown out, but most of the art I think we could keep.
Content_zh: 所以有些東西可能會被丟掉，但我覺得大部分美術都能保留。

Content: So it won't be a total, like waste, in that sense.
Content_zh: 所以就這層意義來說，不會完全浪費。

Content: So that's usually the trailers work, right in there, you're trying to create an overview of the game,
Content_zh: 預告片的工作通常就是這樣，對吧，你得試著做出遊戲的概觀，

Content: even though a lot of it's not even done or even started on.
Content_zh: 雖然很多事情都還沒完成 甚至開始

Content: Offscreen Voice: So Eliot's on the team for a little while.
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以 Eliot 會暫時加入團隊一陣子。

Content: [Applause] Roberts: I've been secretly working on the trailer for the past week.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Roberts：上星期我一直在暗中做預告片的工作。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Commercial?
Content_zh: 畫外音：廣告？

Content: Crook: That's what you were talking about, this is what you wanted.
Content_zh: Crook：這就是你剛剛說的，這就是你想要的。

Content: The girl to be thinking during this.
Content_zh: 女孩在這段裡要有所思考。

Content: Schafer: It's like, yes, she's apprehensive.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，她有點不安。

Content: She's like is this really the right thing for me to be doing?
Content_zh: 她像是在想：「我這樣做真的對嗎？」

Content: Everyone's so happy but I'm like, Rice: I think it's good.
Content_zh: 大家都很開心，但我像是……Rice：我覺得很好。

Content: Crook: Is the head scratch too obvious or not?
Content_zh: Crook：抓頭會不會太明顯？

Content: Schafer: No, I mean, if anything, it's a little, I mean, I guess, I was only worried about reading as a head scratch
Content_zh: Schafer：不，我是說，如果要說的話，有點……我是說，我只是擔心會不會看起來像抓頭，

Content: and not a finger in the eye, but.
Content_zh: 而不是手指戳到眼睛，不過。

Content: Roberts: And Tim's vision, that, that always helps because he, he knows his characters so well.
Content_zh: Roberts：Tim 的想法也很有幫助，因為他非常了解自己的角色。

Content: Where like working on effects, you work with some directors that have no idea how to interpret animation,
Content_zh: 像是做特效時，你會遇到一些完全不知道怎麼解讀動畫的導演，

Content: so you show them a shot and they're just like, they give you some random note and they're just like, I, I don't really know what to say about this note,
Content_zh: 你給他們看一個鏡頭，他們就隨便給你一條意見，而你會想，我真的不知道這條意見該怎麼辦。

Content: because I don't know what I'm looking at.
Content_zh: 因為我不知道自己在看什麼。

Content: I'm not looking at a finished product and I know what my character should be doing, but I don't know how to express that.
Content_zh: 我看的不是完成品，而我知道我的角色應該做什麼，但不知道該怎麼表達。

Content: And it's surprising how many directors out there are like that.
Content_zh: 讓人驚訝的是，外面有很多導演都是那樣。

Content: But Tim is different because he, he knows his characters so well.
Content_zh: 但 Tim 不同 因為他非常瞭解自己的人物

Content: And he would give notes sometimes that I'd, I remember going into Brutal and getting a note and thinking like, that's crazy.
Content_zh: 有時他會給我一些意見，我記得做《Brütal Legend》時收到一條，心想這太瘋狂了。

Content: I don't think that note's going to work.
Content_zh: 我覺得那張字條沒用

Content: Like that, like Eddie shouldn't be doing what, with that.
Content_zh: 像是 Eddie 不應該那樣做。

Content: And so I, but you know, you have to go animate it.
Content_zh: 可是你知道，你還是得去把它做成動畫。

Content: So I went and animated it and it totally worked.
Content_zh: 所以我去做了，結果完全可行。

Content: It was just like totally looked good for what Eddie Riggs was doing, because he knows his characters that well.
Content_zh: 那看起來完全符合 Eddie Riggs 當下在做的事，因為他就是這麼了解自己的角色。

Content: And it's a really awesome thing.
Content_zh: 這真是太棒了

Content: It's really rare.
Content_zh: 真罕有

Content: Schafer: Thank you, Elliot.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝你 Elliot

Content: I'll come back in tomorrow and change everything.
Content_zh: 我明天再回來把所有東西改掉。

Content: Schafer: So much pressure walking down the hallway, 'cause of the camera.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為有攝影機，走在走廊上壓力超大。

Content: Yeah, out of here, JP, get. Go.
Content_zh: 對，出去啦，JP，走。快走。

Content: LeBreton: But I'm taking this file cabinet and these bananas with me.
Content_zh: LeBreton：但我要帶上這個文件櫃和香蕉

Content: Schafer: It's like "The Jerk" You're like Steve Martin.
Content_zh: Schafer：這很像《The Jerk》，你就像 Steve Martin。

Content: Get out of here ack mac.
Content_zh: 滾開，麥克

Content: Off of Red's with you.
Content_zh: 你也離開 Red’s 吧。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we lost JP.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們失去 JP 了。

Content: And he had to go to another project.
Content_zh: 他不得不去另一個專案。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Do you feel like you got to make any contributions to the game?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你覺得你對遊戲有貢獻嗎？

Content: Were you there long enough?
Content_zh: 你在那夠久嗎？

Content: LeBreton: To Red's?
Content_zh: LeBreton：對 Red’s 嗎？

Content: Yeah, I think so. Yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: 我想是的 是啊，是啊。

Content: Just, you know, in a small, you know, yeah.
Content_zh: 只是，你知道，規模不大啦。

Content: It might be slight, you know, in the grand scheme of things, 'cause like, you know, there's still a bunch of game to make.
Content_zh: 放在整體來看可能很小，因為你知道，遊戲還有很多要做。

Content: But yeah, I feel like I got to be involved and got to know the team, the specific team culture.
Content_zh: 不過對，我覺得自己有參與到，也認識了這個團隊、這個團隊特有的文化。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Brandon had to leave, too.
Content_zh: 畫外音：Brandon 也得走了

Content: Schafer: Brandon had another project that needed him and he's going onto that.
Content_zh: Schafer：Brandon 有另一個專案需要他，所以他要去那邊。

Content: Schafer: I'm out tomorrow so this is our last dailies with you.
Content_zh: Schafer：我明天不在，所以這是你和我們最後一次 dailies。

Content: Good having you.
Content_zh: 有你真好

Content: Dillon: Yeah, this is actually the second game at Double Fine that I've worked on that I've left before it was finished.
Content_zh: Dillon：對，這其實是我在 Double Fine 做的第二款遊戲，也是第二款我在完成前就離開的遊戲。

Content: So yeah, that's, that's always, I think that's where the melancholy comes from more than anything.
Content_zh: 所以對，我想那種憂鬱大多就是從這裡來的。

Content: It's just sort of like, you know, you want to kind of see things through to the end,
Content_zh: 就是，你知道，你會想把事情看到最後。

Content: but my general feeling is that I'd like to work on whatever is the most important thing for me to work on.
Content_zh: 但我總的感覺是 我想做一些最重要的事

Content: LeBreton: But I think it goes to show that, you know, like we're a multi-projects studio through and through at this point.
Content_zh: LeBreton：但我覺得這也顯示出，我們現在徹底是一間多專案工作室。

Content: And that, you know, we can't, putting all of our eggs into one basket, even if it is a very high profile basket, that you know,
Content_zh: 而且，你知道，我們不能把所有雞蛋都放在同一個籃子裡，即使那是一個非常受矚目的籃子，

Content: that a lot of our reputation is riding on, you know.
Content_zh: 很多名聲都押在上面。

Content: It's kind of like, well, Red's, Red's is definitely the future of the studio, but all these other things are, they're sort of further future,
Content_zh: 有點像是，Red’s 當然是工作室的未來，但其他那些東西則是更遠一點的未來，

Content: further into the future at the studio.
Content_zh: 是工作室更往後的未來。

Content: 'Cause hopefully, you know, yeah, what we're obviously all hoping for is that Red's comes out and really fulfills the promise of the original Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 因為希望，對，我們顯然都希望 Red’s 推出後，能真正實現當初 Kickstarter 的承諾。

Content: Because the Kickstarter backlash has begun in earnest, you know, and people are saying like, oh, you know, like these guys, you know,
Content_zh: 因為對 Kickstarter 的反彈已經正式開始了，有人在說，喔，你知道，這些傢伙，

Content: what they have, are they, you know, are these, is this team ever going deliver?
Content_zh: 他們到底有什麼？這個團隊到底會不會交出成果？

Content: Is this team, we haven't even heard from this team and whatever.
Content_zh: 這個團隊是怎樣？我們甚至都沒聽到他們的消息，諸如此類。

Content: So I think, you know, the people who can make that work, the developers who can make that work and the ones who can't are, there's going,
Content_zh: 所以我想，能把這件事做成的人、能做到的開發者，和做不到的人，

Content: those packs are going to break apart pretty soon if they haven't already.
Content_zh: 這些群體如果還沒分開，很快也會分開。

Content: So, yeah, so yeah, we'll see.
Content_zh: 所以，是的，是的，我們看看。

Content: [Music] Schafer: All right. Red's.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：好。Red’s。

Content: Oh yeah, Red's.
Content_zh: 哦，是的，Red’s。

Content: I've got to name that basically today or tomorrow, really.
Content_zh: 基本上我今天或明天就真的得替它取名字了。

Content: And I mean, names change all the time.
Content_zh: 我是說 名字總是變的

Content: Muir: Top three, top three.
Content_zh: Muir：前三，前三。

Content: Schafer: No, no.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，不。

Content: Not going to, Muir: Bottom three, bottom three.
Content_zh: 不會，Muir：倒數三個、倒數三個。

Content: Schafer: We're working on it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們正在努力

Content: Top people are working on it right now.
Content_zh: 最頂尖的人現在正在處理。

Content: We also recorded live music this morning.
Content_zh: 今天早上我們還錄了音樂

Content: Brian went and recorded music for the trailer.
Content_zh: Brian 為預告片錄音

Content: That go OK, Brian?
Content_zh: 好了嗎 Brian

Content: Min: It was nice. It was very cool.
Content_zh: Min：很好 非常酷

Content: [Music] McConnell: That was beautiful.
Content_zh: [音樂] 真漂亮

Content: I think that's our take.
Content_zh: 我想這是我們的選擇

Content: [Music] [Tim Hums along]
Content_zh: [音樂] [Tim 跟著哼]

Content: [Monster roar] Schafer: The one that seems a little out of place is the monster, Maiden's Feast one,
Content_zh: [怪物吼聲] Schafer：聽起來有點不搭的是怪物、少女盛宴那段，

Content: 'cause it seems like it's scored.
Content_zh: 因為感覺它已經有配樂了。

Content: Could I hear just that part again?
Content_zh: 我能再聽一遍嗎？

Content: Min: I mean, it sounds pretty cool to me.
Content_zh: Min：聽起來很酷

Content: We could certainly bring that down if it's a little overpowering.
Content_zh: 如果有點太搶，我們當然可以把它調小。

Content: Schafer: I guess what really jumped out is being different than the rest of it, you know what I mean?
Content_zh: Schafer：我想真正跳出來的是，它跟其他部分不一樣，你懂我的意思嗎？

Content: I like the rest of it, but just that first, that first.
Content_zh: 其他部分我都喜歡，只是那第一個、第一個。

Content: Min: Yeah, yeah, we could, Schafer: That was crazy.
Content_zh: Min：是的，是的，我們可以，Schafer，那太瘋狂了

Content: I love it.
Content_zh: 我喜歡

Content: Awesome. Do it.
Content_zh: 太棒了 快

Content: Min: I'll send that out to you and then you can check it in your box and listen to it a few more times.
Content_zh: Min：我會寄給你，你可以在自己的設備上檢查，再多聽幾次。

Content: Min: Thank you, Cam.
Content_zh: Min：謝謝你 Cam

Content: Stoddard: I want to make sure I get this right.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我想確保我是對的

Content: So the call of the beast he wants up a little and the music down a little, is that right?
Content_zh: 所以獸吼他想要大聲一點，音樂小聲一點，對嗎？

Content: Min: No, I think we should just leave it, because it sounds awesome.
Content_zh: Min：不，我覺得就保持這樣，因為聽起來超棒。

Content: We'll just bounce out this version and then Tim's going to, he's going to obsess over it anyway.
Content_zh: 我們就輸出這個版本，然後 Tim 反正會一直鑽牛角尖。

Content: He's going to just listen to it like five, six times.
Content_zh: 他要聽五六遍

Content: And then if it really bothers him, he will tell me.
Content_zh: 如果他真的覺得困擾，他會告訴我。

Content: So I think it's fine.
Content_zh: 所以，我想它很好。

Content: Camden: The thing I like most about this trailer is the dynamics.
Content_zh: Camden：我最喜歡這支預告片的地方是動態。

Content: The fact that there's quiet and soft parts.
Content_zh: 它有安靜、柔和的段落。

Content: That kind of stuff is rarely seen.
Content_zh: 這種東西很少看到

Content: In fact, a lot of times in audio you're really tempted to have everything the same.
Content_zh: 其實很多時候做音效，你會很想把所有東西都做成一樣。

Content: Like in the same kind of perspective audio-wise.
Content_zh: 像是都用同一種聽覺視角。

Content: So like that stuff, like it's rare to see a game trailer that quiet.
Content_zh: 所以那種東西，很少看到遊戲預告片這麼安靜。

Content: And so I'm really interested to see how this is received, because it's very out of the cut.
Content_zh: 所以我真的很想看看大家會怎麼反應，因為它非常不按牌理出牌。

Content: So I'm, it's very Tim.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得，它很 Tim。

Content: I love how tied in that is, because Tim is, like the central theme is like kind of coming into your own and breaking out of the routine
Content_zh: 我很喜歡這點和主題綁得這麼緊，因為 Tim 的核心主題有點像是找到自己、跳脫例行模式，

Content: and really kind of fighting for your creative soul.
Content_zh: 真正為自己的創作靈魂而戰。

Content: He's like, I got to hand it to Tim.
Content_zh: 我真的得佩服 Tim。

Content: When he makes a story, like it's in there.
Content_zh: 他寫故事時，那些東西都在裡面。

Content: It's deep.
Content_zh: 很深。

Content: And I had a lot of fun, kind of trying to find the audio voice of that.
Content_zh: 而我很享受去尋找那種聲音語言的過程。

Content: Schafer: It's going to be awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：會很棒的

Content: Petty: New music in and you can take a listen and if there's any last things you want, let me know.
Content_zh: Petty：新音樂放進去了，你可以聽聽看，如果還有最後想改的地方就告訴我。

Content: Schafer: Cool. Exciting.
Content_zh: Schafer：酷 令人振奮。

Content: Offscreen Voice: It's almost done?
Content_zh: 畫外音：快完成了？

Content: Petty: Yeah. Like anything, you know.
Content_zh: Petty：對。就像任何東西一樣，你知道。

Content: So this is the way games or trailers or art develops.
Content_zh: 所以這就是遊戲、預告片或藝術發展的方式

Content: It's like you start here and this is where you want to be and you go whoa!
Content_zh: 就像你從這裡開始，想去那裡，然後一路哇！

Content: And then you go, I'm going to spend some undetermined period of time right here getting this last five to ten percent.
Content_zh: 接著你會花一段不知道多久的時間，就卡在這裡，把最後百分之五到十補完。

Content: So really, yeah, we're in that kind of, we're close to that.
Content_zh: 所以對，我們現在差不多就在那個階段，已經接近了。

Content: Ryken: I just saw so many glaring things with the textures that I was like, God!
Content_zh: Ryken：我剛看到材質上有好多很明顯的問題，心想，天啊！

Content: If we're going to work on these guys, we're going to do it now for this trailer.
Content_zh: 如果我們要修這些角色，就是現在為了預告片修。

Content: Just get them as close to where we want them to be, yeah.
Content_zh: 只要讓他們接近我們想要的地方，是的。

Content: Petty: Levi's gone back and touched up some of the, sort of uses it as an excuse also to go in and tighten up some of the earlier paintings.
Content_zh: Petty：Levi 回去修了一些東西，也算是趁這個機會回頭把早期幾張畫收得更緊。

Content: So the girl's actually been completely repainted, as has the boy.
Content_zh: 所以女孩其實已經整個重畫了，男孩也是。

Content: But I mean, it's still the same design and everything, but they're just tighter now and I think will pop off the background a little bit.
Content_zh: 但設計之類的都還是一樣，只是現在更精緻，我覺得也會更從背景中跳出來。

Content: Something that a lot of us talked about for a while.
Content_zh: 我們很多人談過的

Content: Petty: So the, you know, when we, I think we have been with this very thin characters.
Content_zh: Petty：所以，你知道，我覺得我們一直用的是很細的角色。

Content: And I think that makes them, of course, harder to read.
Content_zh: 我想這會讓他們更難看懂

Content: And the other thing was the very first environment that we chose to polish has an analogous color scheme to the main character in that environment.
Content_zh: 另一件事是，我們最早選來打磨的環境，配色和那個環境裡的主角很接近。

Content: Team Member: Yeah.
Content_zh: 團隊成員：是啊。

Content: Petty: A peach-colored thing, but that she's also peach and pink.
Content_zh: Petty：是桃色的東西，但她本身也是桃色和粉紅色。

Content: Roberts: That's where it first stood out to me.
Content_zh: Roberts：那是我最先想到的地方

Content: Gardner: Yeah well done, Master of Color.
Content_zh: Gardner：做得好，色彩大師

Content: Stapley: I like pink.
Content_zh: Stapley：我喜歡粉紅色

Content: I like pink. I like pink.
Content_zh: 我喜歡粉紅色 我喜歡粉紅色

Content: It's your fault.
Content_zh: 都是你的錯

Content: Gardner: Fuck you!
Content_zh: Gardner：去你的！

Content: Stapley: Fuck you man. With your anime shit.
Content_zh: Stapley：去你的啦，老兄，還有你那些動漫鬼東西。

Content: Ryken: I'm excited for this.
Content_zh: Ryken：我很興奮

Content: I think we came a long way figuring out the potential for what all of our scenes can look like with the trailer. Yeah.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們一路走了很遠，透過預告片摸索出所有場景可能呈現的樣子。對。

Content: Just finding something we're happy with. Yeah.
Content_zh: 只是找到我們喜歡的東西 是啊。

Content: Choy: Like for example, this Maiden Feast scene was one of the very first scenes that was kind of put together
Content_zh: Choy：比如說，這場 Maiden Feast 是最早被組合起來的場景之一，

Content: before the sort of art work flow pipeline and tools were even like, were even set.
Content_zh: 那時候美術工作流程、pipeline 和工具都還沒定下來。

Content: So, so going back to this one it was actually like, it was like, whoa, we've come a long way, 'cause it was like organized differently
Content_zh: 所以回頭做這段時，其實就像，哇，我們真的進步很多，因為它的整理方式很不一樣，

Content: and there was a lot of things that could be adapted to like the new workflow.
Content_zh: 也有很多東西可以調整成新的工作流程。

Content: Stapley: In a way, it seems like we're kind of grabbing from this point, you know, and this point and this point and this is where we are now.
Content_zh: Stapley：某種程度上，我們像是從這個點、這個點和這個點各抓一點，這就是現在的我們。

Content: But I mean, like where we are now, it's awesome.
Content_zh: 但我是說，我們現在的成果很棒。

Content: The trailer looks fucking sick.
Content_zh: 預告片看起來超猛。

Content: I think everything's awesome.
Content_zh: 我覺得一切都很棒

Content: So yeah, I'm more stoked on this system.
Content_zh: 所以對，我對這套系統更興奮了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: It seems more like what Tim envisioned.
Content_zh: 畫外音：似乎更像 Tim 的預想

Content: Stapley: Yeah, I hope so, I hope so.
Content_zh: Stapley：是的，我希望如此，我希望如此。

Content: Stapley: If everything with the game could look like that final shot then, people are going to shit, yeah.
Content_zh: Stapley：如果整款遊戲都能像最後那個鏡頭一樣，大家一定會嚇瘋。

Content: Schafer: Yeah. Seeing some of the final stuff, like that final piece of key art when Bagel drew it, it was beautiful
Content_zh: Schafer：對。看到一些最終成品，像 Bagel 畫的那張最終 key art，真的很美，

Content: and it just made me feel really good about how beautiful the game is going to look, you know.
Content_zh: 也讓我很確信這款遊戲會變得很漂亮。

Content: And all the shots in the trailer are looking really good, especially when you see like the key art
Content_zh: 預告片裡所有鏡頭看起來都很好，尤其是當你看到 key art，

Content: and how it looks like the original doodle I had in my notebook.
Content_zh: 它看起來就像我筆記本裡最初的塗鴉。

Content: It's like OK, this is exactly what I kind of imagined it being from the beginning.
Content_zh: 這正是我從一開始就想的

Content: Petty: Logo. It's not there yet.
Content_zh: Petty：Logo。還沒到位。

Content: Schafer: God, what would go there!
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊，那裡到底該放什麼！

Content: The issue is you're always waiting for the perfect name to just pop into your head and it was just time to like, like we got to do it.
Content_zh: 問題是你一直在等完美的名字突然跳進腦袋，但時間到了，我們就是得做決定。

Content: 'Cause in some ways it's kind of silly, 'cause names don't matter in some way as much people think.
Content_zh: 因為某方面來說這有點蠢，名字其實沒有人們想的那麼重要。

Content: Like as much as you think in the moment when you're trying to come up with the perfect name, you're like, oh, that's not perfect.
Content_zh: 當下你想著要取出完美名字時會覺得很重要，看到一個就想，喔，那不完美。

Content: Schafer: Today's the day when the name is due in the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：今天就是遊戲名稱要定案的日子。

Content: Ah, do you want to see my newest charts?
Content_zh: 你想看看我最新的圖表嗎？

Content: So this is just the latest one.
Content_zh: 所以這只是最新的

Content: It started here.
Content_zh: 從這裡開始的

Content: I'm getting into four columns, I don't waste paper.
Content_zh: 我開始塞成四欄了，這樣不浪費紙。

Content: Here' another one.
Content_zh: 這裡還有一張。

Content: And that, and that.
Content_zh: 和那，和那。

Content: I can't delay it anymore, I wish I could.
Content_zh: 我不能再拖延了 我希望我能

Content: I wonder if I can get away with delaying it?
Content_zh: 我在想我能不能混過去，再拖一下？

Content: That Game Informer guy will be really mad.
Content_zh: 那個 Game Informer 的人會超生氣。

Content: So will Greg, because it's dumb to put it in the trailer and announce the game with a code name.
Content_zh: Greg 也會，因為把代號放進預告片、用代號宣布遊戲很蠢。

Content: Ah, I should pick something.
Content_zh: 我該選點東西

Content: What's the least offensive one?
Content_zh: 哪一個最不冒犯人？

Content: Probably Broken Age.
Content_zh: 可能是《Broken Age》。

Content: Schafer: So try on this name, Broken Age.
Content_zh: Schafer：那就試試這個名字吧，《Broken Age》。

Content: Well, we're announcing with the game Broken Age.
Content_zh: 好，我們要用《Broken Age》這個遊戲名稱來宣布。

Content: And I think, I think it's a good name.
Content_zh: 我覺得這是個好名字

Content: It stands for the broken era in which these kids live and their awkward age that they live in.
Content_zh: 它代表這些孩子生活的破碎時代，也代表他們所處的尷尬年紀。

Content: And how they're breaking out of these cycles.
Content_zh: 以及他們是如何突破這些週期的

Content: So I think it's definitely an appropriate name.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得這絕對是個合適的名字。

Content: And it's really hard to tell if it, like if it, 'cause it's always going to feel a little weird to change names like this.
Content_zh: 而且真的很難判斷它到底如何，因為這樣改名字總是會覺得有點怪。

Content: And so I'm saying we just put it on and walk around with it.
Content_zh: 所以我是說，我們就把它穿上身，先到處走走看。

Content: And learn to love it.
Content_zh: 學會愛它

Content: And if we learn to hate it, we'll just change it.
Content_zh: 如果之後變得討厭它，我們就再改。

Content: We've changed names before and no problems.
Content_zh: 我們以前改過名字 沒問題

Content: No big deal.
Content_zh: 沒什麼大不了的

Content: But I think it's a very promising name that in two months we'll either head over heels in love with it or we'll have a different name.
Content_zh: 但我覺得這是個很有潛力的名字，兩個月後我們不是會徹底愛上它，就是會換成另一個名字。

Content: So, so that's the name of the game.
Content_zh: 所以這就是遊戲的名字

Content: Stapley: You guys like the name? Yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Stapley：你們喜歡這個名字嗎？是啊，是啊。

Content: Yeah, we were talking about it yesterday.
Content_zh: 是的，我們昨天談過了

Content: I was like, I remember when we thought that the name Red's was weird.
Content_zh: 我還說，我記得我們以前也覺得 Red’s 這名字很怪。

Content: The nickname was weird.
Content_zh: 外號很奇怪

Content: We're like Red's? But now it's Red's.
Content_zh: 我們會說 Red’s？但現在它就是 Red’s。

Content: Like, we didn't think about it.
Content_zh: 我們沒想過

Content: So that's probably going to be the same thing, maybe.
Content_zh: 也許這也是同樣的事

Content: Petty: Going back through all the scenes that we made some advancements in for the trailer and pulling in some of those changes.
Content_zh: Petty：我要回頭把所有為預告片推進過的場景整理一遍，帶入那些改動。

Content: Then I'm off to GDC.
Content_zh: 那我要去 GDC 了

Content: Schafer: Hey, Scott Campbell!
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，Scott Campbell！

Content: Oh it's his dad too.
Content_zh: 喔，他爸爸也在。

Content: That's awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了

Content: One second. Hey, Scott's dad.
Content_zh: 等一下。嘿，Scott 的爸爸。

Content: Schafer: Hi, have you been here before?
Content_zh: Schafer：嗨，你以前來過這裡嗎？

Content: Scott's Dad: Oh, I sure have.
Content_zh: Scott's Dad：是的

Content: Schafer: Welcome back. You've been here many times.
Content_zh: Schafer：歡迎回來 你來過很多次了

Content: Scott's Dad: Soon after you moved, first moved in.
Content_zh: Scott's Dad：你搬家後不久 就搬進來了

Content: Schafer: Oh my gosh.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊

Content: Hey. What are doing?
Content_zh: 嘿 幹什麼？

Content: What are you guys doing?
Content_zh: 你們在幹嘛？

Content: Campbell: So hey, is this for you, it's for Molly, right?
Content_zh: Campbell：嘿，這是給你的嗎？是給 Molly 的，對吧？

Content: All right, Molly.
Content_zh: 好吧，Molly。

Content: Schafer: So yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以，是的。

Content: I don't know what else to say, this has been the month of just doing too many things.
Content_zh: 我不知道還有什麼好說的 這月已經做了太多事了

Content: Being really overloaded with stuff.
Content_zh: 超負荷的

Content: Hopefully next month will be, well, I can focus on design and writting stuff instead of all these extracurricular activities.
Content_zh: 希望下個月我可以專心做設計和寫作，而不是忙這些課外活動。

Content: Schafer: Tonight's winners, tonight's winners were chosen in an open nomination process by 700 of the world's top game creators.
Content_zh: Schafer：今晚的得獎者，是由全球七百位頂尖遊戲創作者透過公開提名程序選出的。

Content: Afterwards I hope the team is all excited because it's our trailer being cheered at for the first time and everyone's going to be jazzed
Content_zh: 之後我希望團隊都很興奮，因為我們的預告片第一次獲得歡呼，大家都會很嗨。

Content: so we'll all go out to dinner, hopefully.
Content_zh: 希望我們都出去喫晚飯

Content: And celebrate.
Content_zh: 一起慶祝。

Content: And that should be really a lot of fun.
Content_zh: 這應該很有趣

Content: Unless people boo.
Content_zh: 除非有人在噓

Content: If people boo at the trailer then we'll probably be drinking a lot more than eating.
Content_zh: 如果大家噓預告片，那我們大概會喝得比吃得多。

Content: Announcer: Ladies and gentlemen.
Content_zh: Announcer：女士們、先生們。

Content: We are pleased to begin the second half of our evening and welcome you to the thirteenth annual Game Developers Choice Awards.
Content_zh: 我們很高興開始今晚下半場，歡迎各位來到第十三屆 Game Developers Choice Awards。

Content: Please welcome the host of the evening, President and CEO of Double Fine Productions, Tim Schafer.
Content_zh: 有請今晚主持人，Double Fine Productions 總裁兼執行長 Tim Schafer。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: Hello, everybody. Hi.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：大家好。嗨。

Content: Wow. This podium is…
Content_zh: 哇 這個講臺是……

Content: Schafer: It's great. I mean, it's so weird.
Content_zh: Schafer：很棒。我的意思是，這真的很奇怪。

Content: It's so different than any other video or anything they're going to show tonight.
Content_zh: 它和今晚要播放的其他影片或內容都很不一樣。

Content: You either have goofy mega-64 videos, you have these amazing triple "A" crazy games and then you have this little hand painted Bagel thing coming out there.
Content_zh: 你們會看到搞笑的 Mega64 影片、那些驚人的 3A 瘋狂大作，然後是我們這個小小的 Bagel 手繪東西跑出來。

Content: I think people will like it. Who knows.
Content_zh: 我想人們會喜歡的 誰知道呢

Content: Schafer: I'm not sure if you heard about this, but a little while back we did a Kickstarter project.
Content_zh: Schafer：不知道你們有沒有聽說，前陣子我們做了一個 Kickstarter 專案。

Content: A game and a documentary.
Content_zh: 遊戲和紀錄片

Content: And we usually, we just keep this stuff just for our backers.
Content_zh: 而且通常，我們只把這些東西留給贊助者看。

Content: By the way, is there, are there backers here in the audience tonight?
Content_zh: 對了 今晚在觀眾裡有贊助者嗎？

Content: Any backers? Yes!
Content_zh: 有贊助者嗎？是的！

Content: Please raise your hand if you're a backer.
Content_zh: 如果你是贊助者 請舉手

Content: Thank you backing and now please put your hand in front of the eyes of the non-backers seated next to you.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們贊助。現在請把手放到旁邊非贊助者的眼睛前面。

Content: For what we're about to show, please enjoy.
Content_zh: 接下來要播放的內容，請欣賞。

Content: [Music] [Cheering and Applause]
Content_zh: [音樂] [歡呼與掌聲]

Content: Schafer: Did you see the trailer?
Content_zh: Schafer：你看到預告片了嗎？

Content: What'd you think of the trailer?
Content_zh: 你覺得預告片怎麼樣？

Content: Looked good, huh?
Content_zh: 看起來不錯吧？

Content: It's great to have this huge payoff, like you don't really often get to crunch in games
Content_zh: 能有這麼大的回報真的很棒，因為做遊戲時不常有機會趕工，

Content: and make something and then hear people cheer for it, so that was great.
Content_zh: 做出一個東西，然後馬上聽到大家為它歡呼，所以那很棒。

Content: We're going to go to Fang, I think, and all have dinner together.
Content_zh: 我想我們要去 Fang，大家一起吃晚餐。

Content: And then see where the evening takes us.
Content_zh: 然後看看晚上要到哪裏去

Content: Right? Right? We're going?
Content_zh: 對吧？對吧？走吧？

Content: Rice: Yeah, we gotta go.
Content_zh: Rice：是的，我們得走了。

Content: Schafer: Let's go right now. OK, bye.
Content_zh: Schafer：走吧 好，拜拜

Content: Wait, I got to find my wife, hold on..
Content_zh: 等等，我得先找到我太太，等一下……

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
